ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-28-2025 Advanced From Jun-17-25 To May-06-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
John Vaught, Esq., and Jack DiAna Esq., for Billie Von LaFever, Jr. (executor).
Kathryn Anderson, for San Francisco Night Ministry (beneficiary).

The court acknowledges the recently filed STIPULATION TO CHARACTERIZE $5000 AS CONDITIONAL GIFT and confirms that the court intends to take the matter under submission to order the $5000 be characterized as a gift. No objection to the preceding is presented.

The matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Thompson. Not Reported.

EX PARTE APPLICATION AND STIPULATION TO ADVANCE HEARING DATE ON FIRST AMENDED FINAL REPORT OF ADMINISTRATION, PETITION FOR APPROVAL FOR ALLOWANCE OF REIMBURSEMENT OF COSTS ALLOWED, FOR ALLOWANCE OF STATUTORY FEES, AND PETITION FOR FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Continued To Jun-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John DiAna appeared by zoom for Billie Von LaFever, Jr. (executor).
Kathryn Anderson appeared by zoom for San Francisco Night Ministry (beneficiary).

Mr. DiAna stated that the notice of appeal was filed on June 12th. Discussion was held regarding the issue of whether the notice of appeal means an automatic stay of the proceeding and the possibility of Mr. DiAna needing to file an amended petition. The First and Final Account and Report of Administration, etc. is continued to June 17, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the appeal.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Petition For Removal Of Executor Billie Von Lafever, Jr.
Off Calendar May-14-2024 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-14-2024 Continued To Jun-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John DiAna appeared by zoom for Billie LaFever.
Kathryn Anderson appeared by zoom for San Francisco Night Ministry.
Trent Thornley (Executive Director of Night Ministry) appeared by zoom.

Mr. DiAna stated that he intends on amending the petition to comply with the court's statement of decision, but asked the court make it contingent upon a notice of appeal not being filed. Mr. DiAna also stated that if the executor has to file an amended petition, it would be a waste of time because it will have to be amended again. Mrs. Anderson raised the issue of the parties meeting and conferring regarding to accounting and stipulating to the first amended petition, and asked that the petition for removal be taken off calendar. Further discussion was held with respect to how to matter would proceed depending on whether an appeal is filed. The First and Final Account and Report of Administration, etc. is continued to June 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Removal of the Executor is off calendar at the request of the moving party.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-17-2024 Continued To May-14-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack DiAna appeared by zoom for Billie Von Lafever, Jr.
Jeffrey Leow and Kathryn Anderson appeared by zoom for San Francisco Night Ministry.
Trent Thornley (Executive Director of S. F. Night Ministry) also present by zoom.

The court noted that Mr. Vaught was not present. Mrs. Anderson stated that she has no current plans to reply to the objections. Mr. Leow stated that if the court favors a reply, they will provide one and that they will confer about that. Mr. DiAna stated that he doesn't think there are any grounds to remove the executor. Discussion was held as to whether it is appropriate to continue the matter, Mrs. Anderson's position that she has requested information and has not seen anything, Mr. Vaught having been the one who filed the objections and not being here, Mr. DiAna's position that an e-mail was provided on April 13th with statements, whether an amended petition will need to be filed, etc. The First and Final Account and Report of Administration, etc., and the Petition for Removal of Executor are continued to May 14, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Guillory. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-13-2023 Continued To Feb-09-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Kathryn Anderson appearing in person for and with Knights Ministry of San Francisco and Trent Thornely (executive director).
John Vaught appearing via zoom for Billie Von Lafever Jr. (petitioner) and Grand Ducal.
John DiAna appearing via zoom for Billie Von Lafever Jr. (beneficiary).
Jim Buiel (decedent's brother) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

Discussion was held regarding the declaration that was filed by Kathryn Anderson in connection with the evidentiary hearing of Sep. 8, 2023. The Petition to Interpret Last Will and Testament of Alexander F. Buiel II is now deemed unsubmitted and reset for a further evidentiary hearing on February 9, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Opposition to the declaration is to be filed by January 17, 2024. A response is to be filed by January 31, 2024. The accounting petition and the petition for removal of Executor are also continued to February 9, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Continued To Oct-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Interpret Last Will And Testament Of Alexander F. Buiel
Off Calendar Jun-21-2023 Continued To Sep-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Catherine Anderson appearing in person for and with Knights Ministry of San Francisco and Trent Thornely (executive director).
John Vaught appearing via zoom for Billie Von Lafever Jr. (petitioner) and Grand Ducal.
John DiAna appearing via zoom for Billie Von Lafever Jr. (beneficiary).
Jim Buiel (decedent's brother) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to what the descendant's intent was, whether the gift to Knight Ministry is valid, the issue of the interpretation of the will being a matter for the Court to decide, the issue of whether an evidentiary hearing is appropriate, whether ademption applies, whether the account to where the funds were transferred are part of the residuary contents of the will, etc. Upon conclusion of argument the Petition to interpret last will and testament is set for an evidentiary hearing on September 08, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (no more than two hours).

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

06/21/2023:

The First and final account etc., and the Petition for removal of executor are continued to October 18, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (the assigned probate examiner will notify the parties).

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-19-2023 Continued To Jun-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Interpret Last Will And Testament Of Alexander F. Buiel
Off Calendar Apr-19-2023 Continued To Jun-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Executor Billie Von Lafever, Jr.
Off Calendar Apr-19-2023 Continued To Jun-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kathryn Anderson for San Francisco Night Ministry's.
Reverend Trent Thornely (Executive Director of Night Ministry's) also present.
John DiAna appeared by zoom for Billie Bon Lafever (executor/petitioner/beneficiary).
Jim Buiel (decedent's brother) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court stated that it was unclear as to the sequence of events that occurred for the 401K account at Gordon & Rees which was rolled over to the vanguard IRA in terms of what was done with the monies that were involved, questioned if there was any co-mingling of accounts, and stated that the reference to other accounts at vanguard is unclear. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions and discussion was held with regard to whether or not it was the decedent's intent for the funds in the vanguard accounts to go to San Francisco Night Ministry's, whether or not the doctrine of ademption applies, what the decedent's expressed intent was, the issue of whether or not the gift fails, the relationship of the decedent with Night Ministry's at the time of his death, the relationship of the decedent with Billie Lafever (his best friend), the council that was founded by the decedent, the issue of the decedent having been a paralegal and having to infer some intent as what he did and did not do, the 401K only having $393.00 in 2020, language in the will, the money having been transferred two years before he died, there being no beneficiaries on the vanguard accounts, the residue clause in the will and the conduct of the testator, the three vanguard accounts not being accounted for in the will, whether it makes sense to set up a briefing schedule and the Grand Ducal Council having the opportunity to be heard on this, etc. The court stated that it did not have a chance to review Mrs. Anderson's filing, asked Mr. DiAna if he wanted to file a response, and stated that it wants a briefing schedule. The Petition for Removal of Executor Billie Von Lafever, Jr., the Petition to Interpret the Last Will and Testament, and the First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. are continued to June 14, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. DiAna to respond and file his brief by May 17, 2023. Mrs. Anderson is to file her reply by May 24, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Continued To Sep-28-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John DiAna, via Zoom, for Billie Lafever, Jr., Executor.

Counsel reports of delays caused by issues obtaining financial information from Vanguard investments and acknowledges that the Inventory and Appraisal and an accounting are pending matters. Counsel requests a sixty-day continuance and advises that if an accounting petition is not ready to file by the next calling of this matter, he intends to file a petition for approval of status of administration. Counsel advises that his client and he intend to schedule a call with the financial institution to request the specific financial information he seeks. The court voices its concern that there have been no filings in this matter within the last few months.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Sep-28-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Lydia Villarreal. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Continued To Jul-06-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Deborah Taylor, via Zoom, for Billie LaFever, Personal Representative.

The court asks counsel for an update regarding the status on close of this matter. Counsel points to attorney John DiAna's declaration filed on May-12-2022 advising the court that additional assets may have been discovered which will require the filing of a corrected inventory and appraisal and a cause a delay in filing a petition for final distribution. The court reminds counsel that it expects the filing of either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition to Approve Report on Status of Administration before the next calling of this matter.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Jul-06-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

This matter was put on for hearing with no appearance required for the purpose of admitting the will. No one appeared to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated May 13, 2013 is admitted to probate. Billie Von LaFever, Jr. is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for May 16, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed by May 09, 2022, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Robert Woods; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Not Reported. (D204)
